title: "rescue()"
description: "rescue (alias `catchError`): replace an upstream ERROR with a recovered value. On source DATA → forward; on source ERROR → emit `recover(err)` as DATA (if `recover` throws, forward THAT as ERROR); on source COMPLETE → COMPLETE. After recovery the source is terminal-errored (dead) so no further values flow — the recovered value is the final cached value (matches the frozen pure-ts reference; it does NOT auto-COMPLETE, RxJS-`catchError(()=>of(x))` divergence, flagged)."

<!-- generated by website/scripts/gen-api-docs.mjs; do not edit -->

rescue (alias `catchError`): replace an upstream ERROR with a recovered value. On source DATA →
forward; on source ERROR → emit `recover(err)` as DATA (if `recover` throws, forward THAT as
ERROR); on source COMPLETE → COMPLETE. After recovery the source is terminal-errored (dead) so no
further values flow — the recovered value is the final cached value (matches the frozen pure-ts
reference; it does NOT auto-COMPLETE, RxJS-`catchError(()=>of(x))` divergence, flagged).

## Import

```ts
import { rescue } from "@graphrefly/ts/operators";
```

## Signature

```ts
function rescue(recover: (err: unknown) => S): Operator<S, S>
```

## Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `recover` | `(err: unknown) => S` | Missing @param recover JSDoc. |

## Returns

`Operator<S, S>`
